events.description: Malgerius de Rokesle associated with Rochelei/Ruxley, Kent, in Domesday-era evidence — earliest broad Rokesle name-form currently in the research corpus, but treated as a separate Kent cluster rather than an ancestor of the south-east Ireland line
Godfrey de la Rochelle demesne sub-tenant under Geoffrey de Mandeville Henry I 1100-1135 — earliest currently documented Rokele in Essex
Humphrey de Rochella in the Pipe Roll 31 Henry I, Dorset Danegeld pardon — earliest currently traced Rochella/Rokele-form entry in the English Pipe Roll corpus
Brook Street Hospital charters where William Doo / D'Ou / de la Rochelle / de la Rokele uses interchangeable bynames — keystone for dual-name convention
William 'La Rokella' receives 4 livres for 20 measures of grain — definite-article Anglo-Norman locative form
William Doo / D'Ou / de la Rochelle / de la Rokele d.1198 South Ockendon — bridge generation between Henry I Godfrey 1100-35 and William II d.1226
Heir of William de la Rokeles in royal wardship to Philip d'Aubigny — terra de Willinghal' valet x.l.
Justiciar Sir Richard de Rupella horse-purchase mandate Pat 30 Hen III — earliest currently documented Rokele in Ireland
Escheat of Justiciar Richard's lands — death-date corrects DIB 6 Dec 1276 → ~early Apr 1276
Philip de la Rokele English livery post-homage
Irish-livery writ to Robert de Offord Justiciary of Ireland for Connacht/Wicklow
Margery de la Rokele IPM names patrilineal stem Richard→Robert→John+Margery TNA C 133/54/14
Margery escheat Adlangport+Fydoun to Hugh de Bruges valet at the extent
Philip de la Rokele d.5 Oct 1295 Canterbury
Maud le Brun livery (post-stepfather Richard de Chigewell wardship cascade) escheator beyond Trent Walter de Gloucestria CCR Vol IV p.453 mb.7
John de la Rokell holds Fidon-in-Croc by suit of court at Ballybramoth — verbatim Burtchaell 1314 Iverk extent
William de la Rokell holds Kelroske — verbatim Burtchaell 1314 Iverk extent
Richard Dodde × Richard de la Rokelle agreement — Dodde to aid Rokelle's claim to Dunmore
William de la Rokele acknowledges receipt of money from James 2nd Earl of Ormond
John Rocket attested as Mayor of Waterford — important late-medieval civic-office anchor for the surname in the city
Robertus Poer Fitz-Piers 25 Aprilis Athloypot — Co Waterford Rolls Office Inq Eliz I entry No.6
Redmundus Rockett IPM 3 Jan 37 Eliz I item 40
Livery Nicholas Rocket of Atlongport son+heir of Redmund — Fiant 6749 verbatim Atlongport spelling
Manor of Kilmeaden IPM at RuckellsCastell 4 Jas I
Jacobus Rockell d.3 Apr 1620 Tallow — IPM 24 Jul 1620 — latest currently traced patent-class Rockett proprietor
Nicholas Rockell IPM names heir Edward Rockell aged 16 + 1619 Stephen Strange mortgage
Edwardus Rockall IPM names widow Maria Rockall + father Jacobus d.3 Apr 1620 — latest currently traced chancery appearance under Charles I

Civil Survey VI 1640 + Vol I Tipperary 1640 records no Rockett surname in the checked Civil Survey corpus — evidence favours a Stuart-mortgage displacement pathway over a simple 1652 Cromwellian explanation
Wm Power of Rathgormuck Protestant = redemption-leaseholder on Nicholas Power parcels — recusant-trustee shield blocking Cromwellian forfeiture under 30 Nov 1654 Council Order
6 Rockwell/Rochwell sub-tenant households Pender 1659 Co Waterford Upperthird Principall Irish Names — on Power Fitz-Piers estate under Wm Power Protestant trustee
Richard Strang of Rockwells Castle signs Walsh Loyal Remonstrance as Catholic recusant gentleman
Petrus Rocket b.~1720 Ballytarsna Mooncoin = post-medieval patriarch in this working reconstruction (NLI vtls635311 p043 + vtls635313 p100)
